name: Shipping / Tanker Rates
summary: Day 84 (Friday May 22). **FRONTLINE Q1 RESULTS MAY 22 — AVG DAILY SPOT TCE Q1 $103,500/DAY FOR VLCCs; Q2 CONTRACTED TCEs $181,700/DAY WITH 82% COVERAGE = +75% SEQUENTIAL. CEO LARS BARSTAD ON CALL: 'HORMUZ CHAOS SUPERCHARGED TANKER PROFITS.' ATLANTIC-TO-PACIFIC SHARE OF VLCC VOLUMES NOW 35% (VS 22% JAN-FEB 2026 BASELINE) — TON-MILES DISLOCATION OPERATIONALLY VALIDATED AT FUNDAMENTAL SCALE. LLOYD'S LIST: 'HORMUZ CRISIS SLASHES VLCC VOLUMES BY 36% BUT VOYAGES ARE LONGER.'** **Frontline -3.0% 1D / BOAT -2.3% 1D = PAPER-PHYSICAL BIFURCATION REVERSED DIRECTION, INTENSIFIED. Paper sold the deal anticipation; physical confirmed the cycle peak.** **Paper tape**: Frontline -3.0% 1D, BOAT -2.3% 1D = deal-anticipation paper unwind; same logic as energy: if Hormuz reopens, ton-miles compress, day rates fall. **Physical fundamentals (DIAMETRICALLY OPPOSITE)**: Frontline Q1 spot TCE $103.5K/day → Q2 contracted TCE $181.7K/day (+75% sequential) for VLCCs with 82% coverage (Manila Times / GlobeNewswire May 22 primary); 36% volume cut at extended voyage distances re-prices freight market STRUCTURALLY (Lloyd's List May 22 'Hormuz crisis slashes VLCC volumes by 36% but voyages are longer'). **The cheniere-energy edge (XLE-Frontline-Cheniere thesis) is operationally validated on the Frontline axis** — Cheniere Q1 dividend $0.555/share paid May 19 + $1.2B deployed Q1 (consistent with sustained capital deployment off elevated EBITDA guide). **Paper-vs-physical bifurcation that peaked May 14 has now resolved IN FAVOR OF PHYSICAL** — the Q2 guide is for ACTUAL contracted business, not a forecast. Same pattern as the May 14 Brent paper-physical bifurcation, now in shipping. **Reversibility**: Paper unwind (-3.0% Frontline today) reverses on Pakistan-track failure or Israeli unilateral kinetic action. **Physical fundamentals (Q2 TCE $181,700/day) are CONTRACTED and don't reverse** — they print as Q2 earnings regardless of where Brent goes by August. **AWRP ~1% holding from May 19 levels** (8x pre-war) — insurers have NOT bought the deal-anticipation tape yet; physical premium intact. PRIOR (May 19 — Monday): **BOAT $41.36 -2.2% / Frontline $36.66 -1.1% / +20.0% 3M** carries forward from Friday closes. Markets dossier: Evercore tanker downgrade on 2027-28 oversupply remains driver (~70% crisis-unwind). **B5 VLCC TD3C and AWRP — no fresh data through Monday morning IST.** Baltic Exchange Week 18 partial WS408.13 ($370-410K/day estimated, ~9x pre-war); Week 19 not yet released through Monday. Marine war risk premiums hold at 1.5-3% (some 10%) Gulf H&M — ~8-20x pre-war 0.1-0.15%; JWC high-risk-zone designation unchanged. **Watch Tuesday-Wednesday for Baltic Exchange Week 19 print** — sustained move above WS500 / >$400K/day = operational confirmation of the Brent-paper move. **The Brent $111.47 (+2.0%, StanChart $130-145 path activates) is the bid signal for the physical-shipping leg** — if Tuesday strike order, BOAT/FRO snap back violently with rate-spike thesis confirmed. **Paper-physical bifurcation continues within FRO**: equity selling on Evercore 2027-28 oversupply downgrade while spot VLCC TD3C at $370-410K = paper-physical bifurcation playing out within same company's price discovery. **Houthi Red Sea Day 7 of 7 negative-space window CLOSES UNTRIPPED** today — no second incident since May 11 Hodeidah. **PRIOR (May 17): MARKETS CLOSED SUNDAY — OPERATIVE PRICES ARE FRIDAY MAY 15 CLOSES: BOAT $41.36 (-2.2% 1D, +1.3% 1M, +8.7% 3M); Frontline $36.66 (-1.1% 1D, +4.3% 1M, +20.0% 3M).** Per markets dossier C11: 'Frontline (Tankers) -1.1% 1D despite VLCC TD3C ~9x pre-war' — Evercore ISI downgrade May 13 citing 2027-28 orderbook oversupply. **Paper-physical bifurcation WITHIN THE SAME COMPANY'S PRICE DISCOVERY** — Frontline equity SELLING vs Frontline tanker-day-rate SPIKING. Either equity is the leading indicator of de-escalation (rate spike short-lived) OR the rate spike continues and Frontline equity is the underpriced trade of the cycle. **A6 VLCC TD3C BALTIC EXCHANGE WEEK 18 PARTIAL WS408.13 (~9x pre-war) carries forward as the most recent rate print; Week 19 data NOT yet released through May 17 morning.** **No fresh UKMTO advisory through May 17 morning IST**; no second commercial-vessel seizure since May 14 Hui Chuan + Haj Ali. **Monday May 18 binary**: VLCC TD3C Week 19 release — sustained above $200K/day for 5+ sessions = paper-physical bifurcation terminal-escalation trigger fires. **If NYT preparation report operationalizes Monday, BOAT and FRO snap back violently with the rate-spike thesis confirmed.** **PRIOR (May 16):** VLCC TD3C BALTIC EXCHANGE WEEK 18 PARTIAL PRINT IS THE MOST RECENT (WS408.13, ~9x pre-war); WEEK 19 DATA NOT YET AVAILABLE THROUGH MAY 16 MORNING**. The paper-physical bifurcation that peaked May 14 (VLCC TD3C $467K/day Week 17 vs paper-Brent $105.68 then) has RESOLVED VIOLENTLY into physical confirmation on May 15: Brent closed $109.26 +3.3% / WTI $105.42 +4.2% (WTI booked +11% weekly gain — cleanest week of physical-tightness reflection in cycle). **A7 No fresh UKMTO advisory through May 16 morning** on Hui Chuan or Haj Ali; Iran has not commented; no attribution on Haj Ali; India MEA 'unacceptable' carries forward. No second commercial-vessel seizure near Fujairah or in Qatari EEZ. **A8 SELECTIVE HORMUZ REOPENING — Iran allowed 30+ Chinese ships to transit since May 13 night under 'Iranian management protocols'** (REPORTED Fars/IRGC-Navy primary; Iran state TV; Xinhua; Al Jazeera; CBS; Defense Post; Newsweek). Defense Post and Newsweek noted Iran has occasionally allowed Chinese ships even during the broader blockade because China is the largest buyer of Iranian oil. **Frontline FRO $36.66 (-1.1% 1D, +4.3% 1M, +20.0% 3M)**: equity stayed sold on de-escalation conviction even as physical-shipping rates stayed elevated. **BOAT ETF $41.36 (-2.2% 1D, +1.3% 1M, +8.7% 3M)** — sector-wide unwind despite physical VLCC strength. **Container Freight FBX Asia-NEur ~$2,850/FEU (+10% week); Shanghai-Jebel Ali $8,000+ (4x pre-war) per Freightos May 12** — Hormuz a 'regional' not systemic container shock per Freightos. **Marine War Risk (Gulf): 1-5% H&M (some quotes 5-10%); ~8-10x pre-war 0.1-0.15% — JMIC May 4-5 Hormuz Critical; 6 transits May 3 / 5 transits May 4 vs 138 historical.** LMA: 88% of underwriters retain Gulf appetite; safety, not availability, halts transit. **The Aramco ~30/day Hormuz transit benchmark is now partially explained by Iran-permitted Chinese transit — non-Iranian non-Chinese transit rate is likely LOWER than 30/day headline.** PRIOR (May 15): **A10 VLCC TD3C TANKER RATE NOW CONFIRMED VIA BALTIC EXCHANGE WEEK 17 AT WS459.28 / TCE $467,408/day** (CONFIRMED — Baltic Exchange Week 17 Tanker Report independent operational primary). Week 4 (late January) had TD3C at WS126.5 / TCE $112,394/day = today's rate is ~4.2x late-January reading and ~12-15x pre-war ~$30-40K range. **Week 18 partial data shows WS408.13 (Lloyd's: 'steepest one-day plunge in over half-decade')** — note the decline but rate still ~9x pre-war. **The 'VLCC TD3C sustained above $200K/day for 5+ sessions' trigger added yesterday at 'watching' is now BASIS CONFIRMED via Baltic Exchange ($467K is far above $200K threshold); 5-session sustain remains pending daily-print verification — recommend `watching` upgrade to `confirmed_basis_pending_sustain`**. **C3 FRONTLINE $37.07 (-0.3% 1D, +4.5% 1M, +21.3% 3M) — cross-section confirmation: equity is selling on de-escalation conviction (Evercore ISI 2027-28 oversupply downgrade was immediate cause of yesterday's -3.5%) while spot rate is at multi-year highs. The two are pricing different worlds; one is wrong.** BOAT ETF $42.28 (-0.3% 1D, +3.0% 1M, +11.1% 3M). **Freightos FBX01 China-USWC ~$2,127/FEU +4% WoW; Transpac +$1,000/FEU vs pre-war.** Marine war risk (Gulf): ~1.0% of H&M value (some transits 0.8% post-NCB), ~8x pre-war 0.10-0.15% — premiums now quoted in % of hull value, not bps; Hormuz 'CRITICAL' — 5-6 transits/day vs 138 historical. **CENTCOM redirect tally unchanged at 61.** PRIOR (May 14): Frontline -3.5% / Evercore ISI downgrade / paper-physical bifurcation within same company. Evercore ISI downgrade May 13 citing 2027-28 orderbook oversupply against **SPOT VLCC TD3C ~$420-462K/day per Lloyd's List May 11 'in unchartered territory'** (vs graph's last-recorded ~$100K = MAJOR ESCALATION SIGNAL). Analyst selling forward oversupply; spot at multi-year highs. **Frontline equity SELLING vs Frontline tanker-day-rate SPIKING = paper-physical bifurcation playing out within SAME company's price discovery — investor should treat Frontline equity as leading indicator of de-escalation if it stays sold; treat VLCC TD3C as leading indicator of physical disruption if it stays elevated.** Per markets dossier H reversibility map: Frontline $32-34 (-9-14%) if MOU signs by May 21; **Frontline $50-55 (+34-48%) if Hormuz remains closed through June — VLCC at $420K supports the upside case**. **NEW TRIGGER (added on brent-crude node): VLCC TD3C sustained above $200K/day for 5+ sessions = paper-physical divergence terminal escalation**. BOAT ETF $42.41 (-0.3% 1D, +3.6% 1M, +11.5% 3M). **Container freight (FBX13): China-Mediterranean $4,179/FEU week ending May 12 (-5% w/w); Asia-N.Europe $2,707/FEU (+11% w/w); Asia-Med $3,850/FEU (+15% w/w) — Mediterranean +15% w/w consistent with Red Sea + Hormuz rerouting persisting.** Tanker-container divergence persists. **AWRP ~1% H&M / 7-day (8x pre-war).** CENTCOM redirect tally unchanged at 61. PRIOR (May 13): Tanker market diverging from container market: FBX13 China/East Asia → Mediterranean $4,179/FEU week ending May 12 (-5% w/w) — capacity caught up; tanker market diverging. BOAT $42.56 (-0.5% 1D, +4.0% 1M, +15.9% 3M). **B17 VLCC AGIOS FANOURIOS I (Iraqi Basra crude, bound Vietnam Nghi Son) REVERSED COURSE BACK INTO GULF OF OMAN AT ~5 KNOTS WITHOUT CROSSING US BLOCKADE LINE — VISIBLE CONFIDENCE-COLLAPSE SIGNATURE EVEN FOR COMPLIANT NON-IRANIAN VLCCs as diplomatic posture deteriorates** (Bloomberg + gCaptain May 11). **A11 ARAMCO CEO NASSER MAY 11: 2-5 ships/day Hormuz (DETERIORATED from May 10 ~6/day), 100M bbl/week lost, 880M cumulative, market won't normalize until 2027 if disruption persists past mid-June** — industry-CEO authority on numbers. **B15 SAFESEA NEHA ATTRIBUTION: May 10 Doha bulk carrier was Marshall Islands-flagged Safesea Neha, US-managed Safesea Group NJ (UN peacekeeping/WFP/GSA logistics); 2nd Safesea vessel hit cycle (Safesea Vishnu March 12 Iraqi waters killed one Indian crew); NO claim; Pravda attribution to 2 Iranian UAVs UNCONFIRMED.** **A9 HOUTHI DAY 80 RED SEA QUIET POSSIBLY BROKEN MAY 11** — UKMTO confirmed multiple explosions near vessel 130km SW of Hodeidah; no claim. AWRP unchanged ~1% H&M / 7-day (8x pre-war 0.1-0.15%) — trigger 'AWRP normalization toward 0.5% by May 16' Day 6 of 10 FAILING HARD. CENTCOM 58→61 redirected + 4 disabled holds (3 redirects in 48h = below early-May pace, Project Freedom paused since May 5/6). VLCC TD3C ~$100K/day baseline; Baltic VLCC US Gulf-China $91,731/day (Lloyd's early May); Frontline secured $76,900/day starting 2026 contract. **Reversibility shifting: paper-market sell-off (Frontline -3.4% 1D on Evercore note) co-existing with physical-market tightening (VLCC U-turn, Hormuz 2-5 ships/day) = PAPER-PHYSICAL BIFURCATION DEEPENED.**

recent_signals.headline: **FRONTLINE +2.9% / BOAT +1.2% — TON-MILES BID INVERSE TO SPOT OIL; HORMUZ REROUTE EXTENDS VOYAGE DISTANCE; STRUCTURAL-BID TRIO LEAD.** Hormuz remains effectively closed (~1-30 vessels/day vs 138 pre-war) → US Gulf/Atlantic crude must travel longer routes to Asia/Europe (Cape rerouting) → ton-miles dislocation → VLCC day rates ELEVATED even as Baltic prints normalize off the Week 17 WS459 peak (now WS133/$117K/day per Week 7 latest visible). Arctic Securities upgraded FRO/DHT/INSW/NAT in April on the 'reopening bonanza' thesis. **The trade**: lower spot Brent (less force-majeure tail) PLUS sustained ton-mile dislocation = tanker stocks unhedged-long. Baker Hughes call: full Hormuz reopen NOT until 2H26. **Frontline's pattern of leading BOAT on Brent-down days is now structurally embedded and is the clearest 'war premium migrated to ton-miles' signal.**
recent_signals.verification: Frontline +2.9% / BOAT +1.2% = CONFIRMED operational tape; VLCC day rates elevated = CONFIRMED (Baltic Exchange Week 7); Hormuz reopen 2H26 = REPORTED (Baker Hughes); structural-bid trio pattern = CONFIRMED operational.

Analyst selling forward oversupply; spot at multi-year highs. **Frontline equity SELLING vs Frontline tanker-day-rate SPIKING = paper-physical bifurcation within SAME company's price discovery.** Per markets dossier H: Frontline $32-34 if MOU signs by May 21; **Frontline $50-55 (+34-48%) if Hormuz remains closed through June — VLCC at $420K supports upside case**. **NEW TRIGGER (on brent-crude node): VLCC TD3C sustained above $200K/day for 5+ sessions = paper-physical divergence terminal escalation.** BOAT ETF $42.41 (-0.3% 1D, +3.6% 1M, +11.5% 3M). Container freight FBX13 China-Mediterranean $4,179/FEU week ending May 12 (-5% w/w); Asia-Med $3,850/FEU (+15% w/w); Asia-N.Europe $2,707/FEU (+11% w/w) — Med rerouting persisting. Tanker-container divergence persists. CENTCOM redirect tally unchanged at 61.
sources: Lloyd's List May 11 (VLCC TD3C $420-462K/day 'in unchartered territory') — primary
recent_signals.verification: Frontline tape = CONFIRMED (yfinance); Evercore downgrade = CONFIRMED (broker primary); VLCC TD3C $420-462K/day = REPORTED (Lloyd's List primary — operationally large figure flagged for Baltic Exchange cross-verification); FBX13 + Asia-Med freight = CONFIRMED (Freightos primary).

recent_signals.headline: BULK CARRIER STRUCK 23NM NE OF DOHA SUNDAY MORNING (NEW — first commercial vessel attack since CMA CGM San Antonio May 5; FIRST inside Qatari EEZ; small fire extinguished, no casualties; UKMTO Warning notice; NO claim of responsibility). Friday May 8 closes still operative for traded prices: FRO $39.51 (+3.6% 1D, +13.5% 1M, +40.1% 3M), BOAT $42.77 (+2.3% 1D, +2.6% 1M, +18.6% 3M), VLCC TD3C ~$100K/day baseline; Frontline IS the trinary-regime trade. CENTCOM precision tally May 9: 58 commercial vessels redirected + 4 Iranian-flagged tankers disabled since April 13 (4th identity not publicly disclosed). Lloyd's List ~6 vessels/day Hormuz transit vs ~60 baseline; ~1,500 vessels anchored. AWRP ~1% H&M unchanged at 6-8x pre-war norm; Joint War Committee Persian Gulf 'high-risk' designation MAINTAINED. Container freight Shanghai-Jebel Ali >$8,000/container (quadrupled). Monday May 11 Asian open will be first re-pricing event for accumulated unpriced news (bulk carrier strike + CENTCOM 58/4 tally + Qatar PM Miami + Bahrain detentions). Expect Brent gap-up $2-4 if no countervailing diplomatic news Sunday.
sources: UKMTO Warning notice (bulk carrier near Doha)
recent_signals.verification: Bulk carrier near Doha = CONFIRMED ACTION (UKMTO primary, multi-source); perpetrator UNKNOWN; CENTCOM 58/4 tally = CONFIRMED ACTION; FRO/BOAT prices = CONFIRMED yfinance
